observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
We recommended and they agreed that Management:
transfer the unexpended cash balance of CY 2024 DDRMF amounting P5,220,483.60 from the General Fund to the Trust Fund; and
direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the corresponding journal entry to record the transfer of the unspent DRRMF from the General Fund.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed that the  Municipal Accountant :
prepare a Journal Entry Voucher to reclassify the heavy equipment totaling P9,990,000.00 to the proper account, from Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment to the Construction and Heavy Equipment account; and
prepare a Journal Entry Voucher to reclassify the food stuff inventory for distribution totaling P1,612,950.00 to the proper account, from Food Supplies Inventory to the Welfare Goods for Distribution account.

Recommendations:
We recommended and  the Municipal Mayor agreed that the:
Municipal Engineer  fast-track the retrieval of relevant certificates of project completion and submit copies to the Municipal Accounting Office for the proper reclassification;
Municipal Accountant:
3.7.2.1	 re-classify the completed projects from the CIP accounts to its appropriate PPE accounts duly supported with certificates of project completion; and
3.7.2.2	 compute for the unrecorded depreciation starting on the year the projects were completed.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed that the:
GSO-Designate include in the GSO’s work plan, the timelines for the conduct of a semestral physical inventory count, the preparation, and the submission of the RPCI to MAO and COA on or before July 31 and January 31 of each year; and
Municipal Accountant  reconcile the inventory accounts and the RPCI, upon submission of the RPCI by GSO and make the necessary adjustments, if warranted, to ensure Management’s assertions on the existence, completeness, and accuracy of the reported balances.

Recommendations:
We recommended and  Management agreed that the  MDRRM Officer and the Municipal Accountant ensure the timely submission of the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF within fifteen (15) days after the end of each month.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed that the:
Local School Board (LSB)  ensure the preparation of an LSB Budget using the Income and Expenditures Estimates form as required under DepEd, DBM and DILG JC No. 1 of 2017; and
Municipal Accountant regularly prepare the Report on SEF Utilization.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed that the  MSWDO ensure the  preparation and submission of the Accomplishment Report as provided for under DBM-DSWD-NCSC-NCDA JMC No. 1, s. 2023, to facilitate the monitoring and evaluation of its performance in the implementation of the formulated plans, programs, projects (PPAs) to address the concerns of the SC and PWD.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Facilitate the reconciliation of the records between the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and Municipal Accounting Office pertaining to taxes receivable and subsequently, require the Municipal Accountant to undertake the necessary adjustments if warranted; and
Establish reconciliation procedures for the recording of the taxes receivables between the Municipal Accounting Office and Municipal Treasurer’s Office.

We recommended and Management agreed to expedite the procedures for the conduct of one-time cleaning of PPE accounts as provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 by undertaking initial inventory taking and recognition of those found at the station.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit report of delinquent public market occupants rentals to the Municipal Accounting Office for the proper taking up in the books of accounts; and
Require the Municipal Accounting Office to record the delinquent public market rental as Account Receivable at the year.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Require the Municipal Treasurer to formulate procedures to allow the proper monitoring of the issued checks and to religiously observe the sending of notices to payees on the stale and returned checks; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting entries relating to the stale checks noted.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Require the Municipal Accountant to adjust the related accounts relating to semi-expendable properties; and
Require the Municipal Accountant, General Services Officer and end-user or accountable officer to implement the guidelines as provided in COA Circular No. 2022-004 dated May 31, 2022.

We reiterated our previous audit recommendation and Management agreed to:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to observe the religious usage of the subsidiary ledger feature of the accounting system to reflect transactions on each bank account;
Require the service provider of the accounting system to undertake enhancements which will facilitate for a command prompt that will require subsidiary accounts at every entry of transactions in the accounting software to facilitate immediate updating of records; and
Coordinate with the service provider in conducting a re-orientation for the accounting staff about the features of the accounting software duly inviting the COA Representatives to facilitate determination of transaction trails and review of the software design.

We recommended  and Management agreed to:
Regularly update the personnel of the BAC about the deadlines set by the GPPB and submit the documents needed within the deadline set; and,
Facilitate trainings of personnel by accredited providers pertaining to the updates on procurement laws and regulations.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the LDRRMC to review and update the LDRRMFIP annually and to include unexpended balances accrued in the Special Trust Fund as source for funding relevant programs, projects and activities.

We recommended and  Management agreed to:
Establish mechanisms that will ensure the observance of the Municipal Treasurer’s Office (MTO) of depositing collections daily or whenever collections reach P10,0000.00, whichever may be deemed appropriate; and,
Expedite the hiring of personnel to fill-up the plantilla position for the Assistant Treasurer and for the Liquidating Officer.

We recommended  the Municipal Mayor and Management agreed to:
Direct the Municipal Engineer to submit the lacking documents to the Municipal Accountant; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant:
to submit the lacking documents to the Audit team; and
to ensure that claims should be supported with complete documentation before certifying the completeness of the disbursement voucher to preclude issuance of notice of suspensions and disallowances in audit.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Require the Municipal Treasurer to devise effective monitoring system of market operations and encourage lessees to pay their rental/stall fees on due date; and,
Strictly enforce the rules and regulations as required in Municipal Ordinance relating to the delinquent monthly rentals of the Public Market by following the provisions of the local ordinance or introduce other measures in matters of collection of rental fees for proper legislative action.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed on the following:
Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a Statement of Cash Flow other than that produced by the accounting software program since variances were already noted in the reporting of line item; and,
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to issue correspondence to the accounting software provider about variances noted in the line items of the Statement of Cash Flows and to necessitate the immediate reprogramming of the software to present an accurate cash flow statement.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed on the following:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the formulation of policies in the determination of collectability of accounts and the provision for doubtful accounts and impairment losses; and,
Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare aging of receivables as the basis for the presentation in the Notes to Financial Statements.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed on the following:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal General Service Officer to facilitate the reconciliation of records through availing of the one-time cleansing of PPE  mandated by COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 to address concerns on the existence and completeness of PPE; and,
Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal General Services Officer to strengthen the internal control procedures in (a) record-keeping; (b) report preparation; and, (c) reconciliation of reports with other offices, through establishment and documentation of these policies and procedures.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed on the following:
Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineering Office to reconcile the variances noted in the project cost recognized and in the Construction in Progress account;
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries in the discrepancies noted after reconciliation; and,
Establish a procedure by which the percentage of completion cost is communicated properly from the Municipal Engineering Office to the Municipal Accounting Office to facilitate proper recognition of project cost in the accounting books,

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed on the following to (a) establish a policy on what should be considered a substantial period of time in the construction of qualifying asset duly considering the nature of the transactions in the Municipality; and, (b) to require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries thereafter.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed on the following:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries in order to correct the erroneous recording of transactions; and,
Require the Municipal Accountant to evaluate the review mechanisms in place in the office in order to foster uniform understanding as to the proper treatment and recording of transactions.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed on the following:
Require the Municipal Accountant to manually prepare a financial statement duly considering the proper classification of line items in the face of the respective financial statements; and,
Direct the Municipal Accountant to send correspondence to the accounting software developer in order to present financial statements in accordance with the existing Standards.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to observe complete disclosures for the collateral of financial liabilities in the Notes to Financial Statements particularly those that are required under IPSAS 30.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to observe the disclosure relating to Disaster Risk Reduction and Management in the Notes to Financial Statements by providing the additional information required by COA Circular 2014-002 pertaining to (a) inventories; (b) property, plant and equipment; and, (c) trust liabilities.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to present only the initial amount approved in the original budget column in the SCBAA; and,
Require the Municipal Budget Officer to establish review mechanisms in the preparation of the SCBAA.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed on the following:
Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to present as Actual Amounts the figures as a result of budget execution; and,
Require the Municipal Budget Officer to establish review mechanisms in the preparation of the SCBAA particularly in ascertaining the Actual Amounts to be presented.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed on the following:
Require the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to review the reconciliation of actual amounts and amounts in the face of the financial statements in the succeeding reporting periods; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer on the provisions in IPSAS 24 on the Presentation of Budget Information in the Financial Statements and IPSAS 1 on the Presentation of Financial Statements for guidance in the preparation of reconciliation statement.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to provide disclosures in the Notes to the Financial Statements on the differences noted in the financial statements.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
Require the GAD Focal Point Personnel to observe the usage of the Harmonized Gender and Development Guidelines (HGDG) tools in GAD planning and to determine the HGDG scores in each activity as a basis for the attribution of expenses for GAD-related activities; and,
Provide corroborative details on reported amounts in the GAD Accomplishment Report to enhance the reliability of reported accomplishments.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management to the Municipal Mayor the following:
Request the accounting software provider to facilitate the modification of the inventory valuation to the moving average method; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to observe the usage of the moving average method in the valuation of inventories and to facilitate identifying interventions, like manual computation of proper inventory valuation, to accurately present the inventory balance in the operational and financial reports pending the modification of the accounting software.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed on the following:
Instruct the Municipal General Services Officer to observe proper recordkeeping of inventories through establishing and documenting procedures and policies of inventory management;
Require the Municipal General Services Officer to facilitate the semi-annual physical count of inventories by coordinating with the Inventory Committee and the Municipal Accounting Office; and
Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal General Services Officer to periodically reconcile their records to detect and correct any errors in the financial and operational reports.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed on the following:
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to remit to the Bureau of Treasury (BTr) the unutilized funds from PDAF and DAP; and,
Require the Municipal Accountant to derecognize such payables about the unitized funds after the Municipal Treasurer has remitted the same to the BTr.

Recommendations:
We recommended the following to the Municipal Mayor:
On the lack of space in the TTMF to cater large number of patients
Direct the Municipal Health Officer (MHO) to coordinate with Barangays in optimizing the use of the Barangay Isolation Units through formulating a referral path when the Municipal Isolation Unit cannot fully cater the number of patients;
Require the MHO to coordinate with other Municipal departments in determining the appropriate number of beds in the LIGTAS COVID-19 Center (TTMF) and to submit a proposal on the physical and manpower requirement for the isolation facility which shall be the basis for determining the appropriate funding requirements.
On the lack of emphasis on differentiating isolation and quarantine
Require the MHO to conduct information awareness activity, in coordination with the DOH, on the difference between isolation and quarantine among: (a) healthcare frontline workers; and, (b) probable, suspected and confirmed COVID-19 positive individuals.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor reassess the policies on the quarantine and isolation of travelers, returning residents and returning OFWs to conform with the existing guidelines.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
Require the Contact Tracing Team to indicate the: (i) time/ date of interview of the suspected cases and listing of close contacts; and (ii) time/ date of the individual was contact traced from the time/ date of interview of suspected case; and,
Reassess the policy in isolation of suspected cases and ensure that immediate mobilization of isolation team is observed to hasten the isolation of suspected cases from the community.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed on the following:
Require the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer (MENRO) to draft policies on: (a) recognition of the threats posed by COVID-19 related health care wastes; and, (b) establishment of separate storage and collection bins for COVID-19 related health care wastes;
Direct the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer to conduct monitoring on each barangay to ensure that COVID-19 related health care wastes are segregated at the household level; and,
Request the Barangays under the Municipality to conduct an information drive to raise awareness of households on proper hygiene practices, and safe handling of infectious and contaminated health care wastes for collection.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
Direct the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer to specify in the guidelines on waste management the important details on the collection and transportation of COVID-19 related health care waste including, but not limited to the following: (a) manner of disinfection of collection and storage bins for COVID-19 related health care waste; (b) schedule of collection of COVID-19 related health care waste; (c) the prohibition of the manual segregation of COVID-19 related health care wastes; and, (d) mandatory wearing of personal protective equipment for sanitation workers; and,
Instruct the Municipal Health Officer and the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer to conduct an orientation to waste collectors on the proper handling of COVID-19 related health care wastes.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed on the following:
Direct the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer and Municipal Health Officer to submit the microbial laboratory tests results of the treatment facility on COVID-19 related health care wastes to the DENR and/ or DOH for approval;
Require the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer to conduct an orientation to waste disposal personnel on the classification of wastes being delivered for precautionary measures, maintaining a safe distance from, and avoid direct contact with the wastes; and,
Facilitate in the establishment of a systemic procedures in the recording and documentation of activities particularly in the recording of COVID-19 related health care wastes that were collected, treated and disposed.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed on the following:
Require the GAD Focal Point Personnel to devise a systematic and holistic approach in the identification of GAD-related PPAs to be undertaken in the period through following the guidelines set forth in the PCW– DILG- NEDA- DBM Joint Circular 2013-01 or through other means that may be deemed appropriate; and,
Instruct the offices in charge of the implementation of GAD-related PPAs to devise a periodic monitoring and review mechanism that would facilitate the determination of the status of implementation of each PPA and to provide a feedback mechanism by which constraints are found in the implementation could be addressed expeditiously.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed to require the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Committee to devise an annual LDRRM Plan that has determinable parameters which are quantifiable, qualitative or time-bounded and by which accomplishments or results of PPAs shall be assessed.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to enjoin the following officials to: Municipal Accountant Maintain separate subsidiary ledgers for the road and its components namely: a) road lot, b) road pavement, c) drainage and slope protection structures, and d) other miscellaneous structures. Reconcile the subsidiary ledger of the road and its components with the Local Road Inventory and Road Map to ensure completeness. Municipal Engineer Segregate and recognize the cost of each component of the local roads with identified cost; Determine the components of local roads identified in the inventory without corresponding cost and recognize the cost of each component at its fair value; Provide the local accountant and the general services officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects. Municipal General Services Officer Maintain a Local Road Network Invemtory and Road Map; and Keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its component.
observations.managementAction: The agency will follow the COA recommendations in order to ascertain existence, completeness and correctnes of the Road Networks account.
observations.reason: Difficulty in reconciliat ion and due to various alternative work arrangement related to the increasing number of cases of COVID-19 in the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and all Department Heads to adhere to the rules and procedures in accounting for supplies and materials provided under Chapter 7 of the NGAS Manual. We also recommended that actual on-site inspection and/or physical inventory be conducted by the Municipality’ s inventory committee to determine the existence of unused supplies/goods as basis in adjusting the expense and inventory accounts and/or recognizing the unused or unconsumed supplies and materials.
observations.managementAction: Recording purchases of supplies, medicines and the likes using the Asset Method Establish Inventory Committee to oversee the conduct of physical count of inventory
observations.reason: Accounting software has system built-in limitations. Inventory committee was not yet established since activities related to COVID-19

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to: Analyze the details of the affected accounts and make the appropriate adjustments; and, For the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the concerned offices for the appropriate action to be undertaken.
observations.managementAction: To prepare adjustments for the affected accounts
observations.reason: Appropriate analysis of the accounts were undertaken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to: Create a team to produce and/or provide reliable documents to support the ownership of the land of the Municipality in coordination with the Municipal Accounting Office, General Service Office, Municipal Assessor’s Office and Municipal Treasurer’s Office; Instruct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Assessor, and the General Service Officer to always reconcile their respective records; and, Register the lands under the Torrents Title System.

We recommended and Management agreed: a. Municipal Accountant: Undertake the proper adjustments of the completed projects to accurately record the PPEs and be lifted out from the Construction in Progress account; b. Coordinate with the Engineering Office of completed projects; c. To provide depreciation of these completed projects which will start on the second month after completion so that the correct value of the projects will be recorded and reported in the financial statements. Municipal Engineer: To prepare and submit to the accounting office the acceptance and inspection report (AIR) and accomplishment report of the said completed projects. Municipal General Services Officer: To provide the municipal accountant the necessary documents for the recognition and transfer of CIP to PPE and facilitate the closing of the completed projects.
observations.reason: Implementation of the recommendations was hampered due to alternative work arrangement.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous recommendation for the management to instruct the Municipal Accountant to draw JEV to restore the cash and liabilities to the stale checks to present fairly the recorded cash-in-bank and accounts payable to the stale checks. Henceforth, strictly adhere Section 59 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs on proper treatment and accounting procedures for stale checks.
observations.managementAction: To prepare the necessary adjusting entries.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following: Refrain from payment of procured goods and/or services through reimbursement basis. All payments should pass through the regular mode of procurement and disbursement procedures in accordance with the provisions of RA 9184 and COA Cirsular No. 97-002; Comply with the requirement of adequate procurement planning in accordance with Section 7 of RA 9184; Submit explanation for purchases made through reimbursement and the lack of valid supporting documents/ (Official Receipt/s) as evidence of payment. Otherwise, the same shall be disallowed in audit; and Consider the setting-up of Petty Cash Fund duly supported by an estimate of expenses which shall be strictly for petty operating expenses in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction: To follow the recommendations and make the proper adjusting entries.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to: Direct the BAC Secretariat to comply with the posting of Notices of Award and Purchase Orders in the GPPB portal as required by the GPPB issuance so that transparency in the procurement process is attained. Direct the Municipal Accountant in ensuring that printout copies of postings of all notices of award and approved contracts or purchase orders be include as one of the supporting documents to be attached to the disbursement vouchers of all procurements made.
observations.managementAction: Direct the BAC to comply with posting requirements and Municipal Accountant to ensure printouts are attached to disbursement vouchers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following: Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of the remaining in-kind donations to ascertain the correct balances; Instruct all Offices concerned to furnish the Accounting Office with all documents pertaining to receipt, distribution and remaining inventory of in-kind donations for costing and recording purposes; and Instruct all Offices concerned to adhere to COA Cirsular No. 2020-9 dated April 9, 2020 to ensure transparency and accountability of donations-in-kind transactions.
observations.managementAction: Conduct inventory count on donations-in-kind received.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to direct the Cashier to strictly limit the disbursements of the petty cash fund for non-recurring petty operating expenses only.
observations.managementAction: To follow the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following: To address the budgetary constraints: Require the Municipal Health Office, Municipal Engineering Office, Municipal Budget Office, and other offices concerned, to identify the funding required after duly considering the necessary activities for the operation of COVID-19 facility; Request the Sangguniang Bayan to provide budget augmentations, if necessary, to find the activities; If there are funding gaps, these shall be submitted to Provincial Government of Davao del Sur for resolution in coordination with DOH, DILG and the DBM as provided in DOH-DILG JAO No. 2020-001. To comply with the Infrastructure Requirements: Instruct the Municipal Health Office to provide for the necessary specifications required in the COVID-19 facility infrastructures; Require the Municipal Engineering Office to provide for: (a) a construction plan duly considering the specifications submitted by the Municipal Health Office; (b) total estimated cost for the facility; (c) the submission of PPMP to the Bids and Awards Committee intended for the procurement of materials; Request the BAC to facilitate the expeditious procurement of the needed materials for the construction. To comply with the Supplies and Other Resources Requirements: Instruct the Municipal Health Office to: (a) identify the supplies and other resources needed for the operation of the COVID-19 facilities; and, (b) submit the PPMP to the Bids and Awards Committee intended for the procurement of materials. Request the BAC to facilitate the expeditious procurement of the supplies and other resources needed for the operation of the COVID-19 facilities. To comply with the Service Requirements: Coordinate with Department of Health, through the Municipal Health Office, providing for mental health and psychosocial support services (MHPSS) in COVID-19 facilities as required under DOH-DILG JAO No. 2020-001.
observations.reason: Delays in the operation were experienced due to the alternative work arrangement; The Municipality is set to comply with the DOH recommendations after the inspection was conducted on the LIGTAS COVID-19 Centers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to consider allocating budget so as to provide food packs to persons with disabilities who have not received any form of assistance from the Municipality during the quarantine period in compliance to the above cited DILG Memorandum Circular. Also, submit explanation or justification why only 3 PWDs were provided with such benefit.
observations.managementAction: All members of the PWD Community were provided with food packs and assistance but only three were named under the member themselves and 643 PWD assistance were named under the head of their families. Also, submit explanation or justification why only 3 PWDs were provided with such benefit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct: The Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documents of the subject transactions and ensure that all payments are supported with proper and complete supporting documents or make a necessary note of any discrepancy before affixing her signature in Box A. The Chairperson of BAC to evaluate and comply with the required documents before recommending approval of the winning bidder.
observations.managementAction: Submission of the lacking documents of the subject transactions and ensure that all payments are supported with proper and complete supporting documents or make a necessary note of any discrepancy before affixing her signature in Box A.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant and MSWDO to immediately submit the e-payroll, SAC form and encoded SAC form/database pursuant to Section 4.6 of PD 1445 and ensure that future/subsequent disbursements of similar benefits are properly supported with complete documents and shall be submitted to concern Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: Submission of the lacking documents pursuant to requirements was undertaken.

observations.recommendation: Once again, we recommended the Municipal Mayor to: Consider to hire/assign additional member/s to the Inventory Committee to assist in the conduct of physical count of PPE; Instruct the Inventory Team to start the inventory taking as early as June 2020; complete the physical count of PPE accounts within a specified time; stick tags in each of the PPE; Require the Inventory Committee to prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE); copy furnished the Accounting Office for reference and submit copy to the COA Office; and Require the GSO and the Accountant to maintain property records; reconcile their respective property records and, make necessary adjustments if necessary.
observations.reason: Software enhancements are still being undertaken. Likewise, reconciliation with GSO and Accounting Office Records was not yet finished due to work arrangement constraints.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant to perform analysis and aging of the loan or refer to the amortization schedule of the loan and present the current and non-current portion of the Loans Payable - Domestic account in accordance with the specific provisions in PPSAS and PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: To reconcile with DBP on the schedule of loan amortization on the schedule of loan amortization.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record the cancellation of stale checks. Henceforth, strictly adhere to Section 59 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs on the proper treatment and accounting procedures for stale checks.
observations.managementAction: To prepare the adjustments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to: a) Direct the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all stallholders have entered/signed contract of lease; and b) Regularly monitor the renewal of stallholder’s lease of contract and strictly enforce rules and regulations in the administration of public market.
observations.managementAction: a. Ensure that stallholders sign and renew contract of lease and renew contracts. b. Monitor the renewal of stallholders' lease of contract and enforce rules and regulations of the public market.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: The management and other officials concerned devise effective monitoring system of market operations and encourage lessees to pay their rental/stall fees on due dates as required by the regulations and agreed in the perfected Contract of Lease; The Municipal Treasurer impose the collection of rental fees and corresponding surcharges from the delinquent stallholders; and The Municipal Treasurer submits report of accumulated delinquent market stall rental income to the Municipal Accountant for proper recording and to reflect amount of receivable in the financial statements.
observations.reason: COVID-19 restrictions were in place limiting movements of personnel

observations.recommendation: We recommended the GAD Council to initiate the establishment of GAD database as required in RA 9710 and JMC No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction: Manual database was already formulated.
observations.reason: GFPS is still establishing a software for an accessible GAD Database

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor: Direct the GFPS to attend trainings and seminars to enhance understanding on GAD Code formulation; and Direct the GAD Focal Point System to assist the Local Sanggunian in the formulation of a GAD Code or Ordinance in a way to give full direction in identifying priority gender issues to be addressed in consultation with local functionaries and stakeholders.
observations.reason: Continuous attendance to trainings was affected; COVID-19 PPAs were prioritized and several trainings were not conducted; COVID-19 restrictions were in-place limiting personnel movements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to: Reconstitute the Inventory Committee who will conduct the physical inventory taking of all Property, Plant and Equipment of the municipality as of December 31, 2018. The Committee shall be given a time frame within which to complete the task; and Create a Committee that will perform reconciliation of records based on the output of the GSO and that of accounting records. The Committee shall be composed of GSO personnel, accounting personnel, and selected personnel from different departments, who shall assist the Accountant and GSO in reconciling records. Likewise, timeframe should be established to ensure that the objective is achieved.
observations.reason: Reiterated this year

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct: The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services Officer to comply with Section 114, Volume I of the NGAS Manual which is to record Inventories under the perpetual inventory method and to maintain their respective supplies/stock/property ledgers and cards;
observations.reason: Reiterated this year

observations.recommendation:
We recommended management to:
• Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to correct the affected accounts;
• Require the Municipal Accountant to recognize the prepaid portion of the insurance premiums paid and to record the Insurance Expenses Account upon consummation of the insurance services every month thereafter, and
• Evaluate the transactions carefully before recording and review the entries of the accounts to avoid errors and misclassification in recording.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant to perform analysis and aging of the loan or refer to the amortization schedule of the loan and present the current and non-current portion of the Loans Payable - Domestic account in accordance with the specific provisions in PPSAS and PD 1445.

Recommendations:
We recommended management to direct the Municipal Accountant to draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record the cancellation of stale checks. Henceforth, strictly adhere to Section 59 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs on the proper treatment and accounting procedures for stale checks

Recommendations:
We recommended the Local Chief Executive to require the OIC - Municipal Accountant to exercise prudence in the recording of financial transactions to ensure proper classification and presentation of accounts in the financial statements in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 and prepare the necessary adjusting Journal Entry Voucher to adjust the balances of the affected accounts.

Recommendations:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
• Direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Accounting Staff – In Charge of SEF Fund to agree on the most reasonable/earlier timelines on the submission of Monthly Report on Real Property Tax Collections to the Accounting Section to give ample time for the review and preparation of remittance documents and to observe the timeliness in the remittance of the provincial share of the SEF collection which is within 15 days of every succeeding month; and
• Consider hiring personnel who have formal studies or training in Accounting to strengthen the review process and equal distribution of work load in the accounting section.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to:
• Direct the Municipal Engineer to adhere to the requirements of COA Circular No. 2013-004 by including in the POW and project design the cost of the public notification/information at the least possible cost or most economically effective means; and
• Henceforth, billboard or signboard having vital information shall be installed at the project site to enhance public awareness and transparency.

Recommendations:
We recommended the Local Chief Executive thru the MPDC that the PPAs appropriated for 20% development fund should be in accordance with the provisions of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1, dated February 22, 2017 to ensure the realization of LGU’s projected results of socio-economic development and environmental management; and re-align the amount of P500,000.00 to developmental projects, thus, the construction of extension classroom shall be included in the SEF annual plan.

Recommendations:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
a) Direct the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all stallholders have entered/signed contract of lease; and
b) Regularly monitor the renewal of stallholder’s lease of contract and strictly enforce rules and regulations in the administration of public market.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive requires the MDRRMO and the OIC-Municipal Accountant to coordinate with each other for the preparation of the Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of the DRRMF and ensure its submission to the Auditor’s office on or before every 15th day after the end of each month, thru the LDRRMC and LDC.

Recommendations:

The foregoing being considered, we recommended the following: Submit list of specific PPAs funded out of the 20% Development Fund appearing in the CY 2016’s AIP as loan amortization – P8,000,000.00, subsidy to barangays - P3,000,000.00, counterpart to national government projects – P5,850,194.00 or a total of P16,850,194.00. Henceforth, the PPAs to be funded out of the 20% Development Fund for the coming years’ AIP shall be specific in order to enhance the transparency and accountability in the Municipality’s implementation/undertaking of development projects.

We recommended that the management consider the following courses of actions: Review the Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan for purposes of ensuring its sustainability, viability, effectiveness and relevance in relation to local and international developments in the field of solid waste management; Monitor the implementation of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan through its various political subdivisions and in cooperation with the private sector and the NGOs; Adopt measures to promote and ensure the viability and effective implementation of solid waste management programs in its component barangays; Establish performance indicators and standards, data recording, reporting and feed-backing systems, responsibility centers to monitor the status of diversion waste implementation; and Intensify the information, education, communication and advocacy campaigns of the Municipality especially on the segregation, reduction, re-use and recycling of wastes to encourage constituents’ awareness on Solid Waste Management Law (Ordinance).

In order to enhance transparency and/or public awareness and monitoring of the procurement process and implementation of awarded contracts of the Municipality, we recommended the following: Require the BAC to prepare the PMR for all types of procurement at the end of each semester and have it approved by the Head of the Procuring Entity; and Submit the duly-approved PMR to the GPPB in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester; copy furnished the Audit Team’s Office.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: 1. Direct the Municipal Accountant and all Department Heads to adhere to the rules and procedures in accounting for supplies and materials provided under Chapter 7 of the NGAS Manual. 2. In addition, we also recommended that actual site inspection and/or physical inventory be conducted by the Municipality’s authorized committee of three to determine if there still existed substantial unused supplies and which the Committee’s Report may be used as basis in adjusting the Expense and Inventory accounts and/or recognizing the unused or unconsumed supplies and materials. 3. Henceforth, all purchases of supplies and materials should be taken up as Inventories under the appropriate account.

We reiterated our audit recommendations in our previous Annual Audit Report that the Employee-in-Charge of the GSO and the Accountant-designate should exert effort to locate the documents/records pertaining to the cost and date of acquisition of the recorded PPEs. If pertinent records could not be obtained despite the exhaustive efforts being exerted to locate them, an appropriate report must be made by the concerned officials to the LCE.

We reiterated our audit recommendation that the Accountant–designate should determine, analyze and reconcile the reciprocal accounts to avoid inaccurate and misleading information. In addition, the necessary adjusting entries should then be made.

Require all officials and employees concerned to fully liquidate their cash advances as soon as the purpose for which they were granted is consummated. Moreover, appropriate action on those accountable officers who failed to comply thereto should be made such as informing them of the consequences that maybe imposed for its non-compliance, like withholding of salary or payment of any money due the accountable officers. The Municipal Treasurer and the Accountant-designate shall closely monitor the above cash advances and send demand letters for the immediate settlement and/or refund of the same.

For the local chief executive to establish a well-defined functional structure on procurement service as to who would be responsible for the submission of Purchase Orders to the audit team within five days from its perfection. The local chief executive, at the time of the exit conference had said to look into the recommendation.
The Accountant-designate shall assess the other investments accounts in the trust fund books to determine the recoverable amount and/or validity and existence.

We recommended that the municipality pre-terminate its existing loans with the LBP. Also, it is recommended that, in the future, consideraton of in-house fund resources prior availing outside fund sources.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations: We recommended that the management consider  the  following
courses of actions:

    1. Review the  Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan  for  purposes  of
       ensuring its sustainability, viability, effectiveness and  relevance
       in relation to local and international developments in the field  of
       solid waste management;


    2. Monitor the implementation of the  Municipal Solid Waste  Management
       Plan through its various political subdivisions and  in  cooperation
       with the private sector and the NGOs;


    3. Adopt measures to promote and ensure  the  viability  and  effective
       implementation of solid waste management programs in  its  component
       barangays;


    4. Establish performance  indicators  and  standards,  data  recording,
       reporting  and  feed-backing  systems,  responsibility  centers   to
       monitor the status of diversion waste implementation; and


    5. Intensify the information,  education,  communication  and  advocacy
       campaign programs of the Municipality especially on the segregation,
       reduction, re-use and recycling of wastes to encourage constituents’
       awareness on Solid Waste Management Law (Ordinance).

Recommendation: We recommended to the Municipal  School  Board  to  strictly
adhere to the mandate and guidelines on the utilization of the  SEF  defined
under RA No. 7160 and DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01  dated  April
14, 1998, in order to maximize the benefits that the students  and  teachers
could avail under this fund.

Recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor  should  direct  the
Municipal  Treasurer  to  observe  timeliness  in  the  remittance  of   the
provincial share of the SEF collection, which is within  15  days  of  every
succeeding month. The Municipal Treasurer should also revisit  RA  No.  5447
to familiarize herself with the duties and responsibilities in the  handling
of SEF collections.

Recommendations: In order to enhance transparency  and/or  public  awareness
and monitoring of the procurement process and implementation of awarded  and
implemented contracts of the Municipality, we recommended the following:

   1. Require the BAC to prepare the PMR for all types of procurement at the
      end of each semester and have it approved by the Head of the Procuring
      Entity; and


   2. Submit the duly-approved PMR to the GPPB  in  printed  and  electronic
      format within fourteen (14)  calendar  days  after  the  end  of  each
      semester; copy furnished the Audit Team’s Office.

Recommendations: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

   1. Direct the Municipal Accountant and all Department Heads to adhere  to
      the rules and procedures in  accounting  for  supplies  and  materials
      provided under Chapter 7 of the NGAS Manual.

   2. In addition, we also recommended that actual  site  inspection  and/or
      physical inventory  be  conducted  by  the  Municipality’s  authorized
      committee of three to determine if  there  still  existed  substantial
      unused supplies and which the Committee’s Report may be used as  basis
      in adjusting the Expense and Inventory accounts and/or recognizing the
      unused or unconsumed supplies and materials.

   3. Henceforth, all purchases of supplies and materials should be taken up
      as Inventories under the appropriate account.

Recommendations:  It  is  strongly  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor
should closely monitor  the  completion  of  the  physical  inventory  being
conducted and ensure the submission of the inventory and  property  reports.


Likewise, the Offices of the General Service and  the  Municipal  Accountant
should maintain inventory and property records in accordance with  the  NGAS
Manual and regularly reconcile their records.

Recommendations:  We reiterated our audit recommendations in  our  previous
Annual Audit  Report  that  the  Employee-in-Charge  of  the  GSO  and  the
Accountant–designate should exert effort to  locate  the  documents/records
pertaining to the cost and date of acquisition of  the  recorded  PPEs.  If
pertinent records could not be  obtained  despite  the  exhaustive  efforts
being exerted to locate them, an appropriate report must  be  made  by  the
concerned officials to the LCE.

Recommendations:   We  reiterated  our   audit   recommendation   that   the
Accountant–  designate  should  determine,   analyze   and   reconcile   the
reciprocal accounts to  avoid  inaccurate  and  misleading  information.  In
addition, the necessary adjusting entries should then be made.

Recommendations:  We recommended that the Accountant determine, analyze and
reconcile the  reciprocal  accounts  to  avoid  inaccurate  and  misleading
information. The necessary adjusting entries should then be made.

The Municipal Treasurer and the Accountant-designate shall closely monitor the above cash advances and send demand letters for the immediate settlement and/or refund of the same.
